blurb_en:
Insta360 turned 11 Thursday and celebrated by announcing Cameraman, an AI agent concept designed to eventually replace the human holding the camera.
• The Cameraman is not a product you can buy. It is a long-term vision — the company says it has already built key technologies for what it calls the robot's brain, eyes, ears, neck, and torso.
• Early prototypes include panoramic drones.
• The Luna Ultra dual-camera gimbal, launched earlier this year, is officially described as an "important intermediate step" toward Cameraman.
• Founder Liu Jingkang published an internal letter about tackling problems "others are unwilling to solve."
• For Insta360 employees: this is the formal articulation of where the company is headed — cameras that film for you without you having to think about them.
• Read more.
blurb_zh:
Insta360创立11周年，发布了"摄影师"（Cameraman）AI代理人概念——让相机终有一天能自己拍摄，不再需要人举着。
• 这不是你能买到的产品，而是长期愿景。官方称已研发出机器人的"大脑、眼睛、耳朵、脖子和躯干"关键技术。
• 早期原型是全景无人机。
• 今年初发布的Luna Ultra双摄云台相机，被官方定位为"迈向摄影师的重要一步"。
• 创始人刘靖康在内部信中写道，要解决"别人不愿解决的问题"。
• 对Insta360员工来说，这是公司方向最正式的宣言：让相机替你拍，不用你操心。
verification_notes: Confirmed via TechNode (July 10, 2026) and cross-referenced with Pandaily and imaging-resource.com. Search result snippets confirmed: AI agent concept named Cameraman, panoramic drone prototype, Luna Ultra as intermediate step, founder internal letter about solving problems others won't. Market cap detail from Pandaily headline not included as specific percentage could not be independently verified from primary source.

blurb_en:
Midjourney is fighting its Hollywood copyright lawsuit by turning the cameras around: the AI image generator filed a motion this week demanding that Disney, Universal, and Warner Bros. reveal exactly how much artificial intelligence they use internally.
• The three studios sued Midjourney last year, alleging its models could generate copyrighted characters like Bart Simpson and Darth Vader on demand.
• Midjourney's defense: "you're doing it too."
• A magistrate judge previously limited discovery to consumer-facing AI products only. Midjourney is asking a higher judge to overturn that ruling.
• The company is seeking internal AI business strategies, training datasets, model weights, and board-level presentations from the studios.
• The irony is structural: studios sued an AI company for unauthorized use of their creative work; that AI company is now asking those studios to expose how they use AI to create. Read more.
blurb_zh:
Midjourney正在反将好莱坞一军：这家AI图像公司提交动议，要求迪士尼、环球和华纳兄弟公开它们内部究竟如何使用人工智能。
• 三大制片公司去年起诉Midjourney，称其AI模型可按需生成巴特·辛普森和达斯·维德等受版权保护的角色。
• Midjourney的辩护策略：你们也在这么做。
• 一名法官此前将取证范围限制在"面向消费者的AI应用"，Midjourney正申请推翻该裁定。
• 他们想要的证据包括：制片公司的AI战略、训练数据集、模型权重以及董事会级别的演示文稿。
• 反讽之处：起诉AI公司侵权的制片公司，现在被要求公开自己的AI使用清单。
verification_notes: Confirmed via TechCrunch (July 4, 2026) and Variety (July 2, 2026), cross-referenced with analyticsinsight, theaiinsider, dataconomy, techbriefly. Key facts: Disney/Universal/Warner Bros. sued Midjourney for copyright infringement; magistrate limited discovery to consumer-facing AI only in June; Midjourney filed motion to expand scope to internal AI practices including strategies, datasets, model weights, board presentations.

blurb_en:
Google Photos launched Video Remix this week, a Gemini Omni-powered tool that turns short video clips into stylized art without leaving the phone.
• Apply watercolor filters, cinematic relighting, background replacement, and oil painting effects to any video clip.
• Limited to clips under 10 seconds — longer recordings need to be trimmed first.
• Requires a Google AI Plus, Pro, or Ultra subscription. Rolling out globally in the US, India, Japan, South Korea, Brazil, Mexico, and others.
• Powered by Gemini Omni, Google's multimodal model that can generate from any input type.
• For anyone in the camera business: Google just embedded an AI creative video transformation layer into the world's most-used photo app. Try it here.
blurb_zh:
本周，Google相册上线Video Remix功能，由Gemini Omni驱动，可将普通短视频一键变成风格化艺术作品，全程无需离开手机。
• 支持水彩滤镜、电影级光效、背景替换、油画效果等多种风格。
• 目前仅支持10秒以内的短视频，超长内容需先剪辑。
• 面向美国、印度、日本、韩国、巴西、墨西哥等地区的Google AI Plus、Pro、Ultra订阅用户开放。
• 由Gemini Omni模型驱动，支持任意格式输入内容生成。
• 对摄像行业的人而言：谷歌已把AI创意视频工具嵌入全球用户量最大的图片App。
verification_notes: Confirmed via TechCrunch (July 8, 2026), cross-referenced with 9to5Google (July 8), Engadget, androidheadlines, Dataconomy (July 9), Techbriefly (July 9), appetizermobile (July 9). Key facts: Video Remix feature in Google Photos, Gemini Omni powered, 10-second clip limit, Google AI Plus/Pro/Ultra subscription required, launched July 8 rolling out globally.

blurb_en:
OpenAI released GPT-5.6 Wednesday in three variants named after celestial bodies: Sol for frontier reasoning, Terra for everyday balanced use, and Luna for fast, cheap work.
• Pricing: Sol at $5 input/$30 output per million tokens; Terra at $2.50/$15; Luna at $1/$6.
• Sam Altman claims Sol is 54% more token-efficient on coding tasks than previous models.
• OpenAI is calling Sol its "strongest cybersecurity model yet" — a description that has appeared on several previous OpenAI releases.
• All three are available across ChatGPT, Codex, and the OpenAI API as of Wednesday.
• The space-themed naming is new. The race to the bottom on model pricing is not. Read more.
blurb_zh:
OpenAI周三发布GPT-5.6，三个版本分别以太阳（Sol）、地球（Terra）和月亮（Luna）命名：Sol负责前沿推理，Terra适合日常任务，Luna速度最快、价格最低。
• 定价：Sol每百万令牌$5输入/$30输出；Terra：$2.50/$15；Luna：$1/$6。
• Sam Altman称Sol在编程任务上的令牌效率比上一代提升54%。
• OpenAI将Sol定位为"迄今最强网络安全模型"——这个描述在多款OpenAI新品发布时都出现过。
• 三款模型已于周三在ChatGPT、Codex和API上全面上线。
verification_notes: Confirmed via Neowin, Dataconomy (July 10), OpenAI Developer Community forum (community.openai.com), iClarified, Engadget, VentureBeat, findskill.ai. Official OpenAI help article: help.openai.com/en/articles/20001325-a-preview-of-gpt-56-sol-terra-and-luna. Key facts: three models (Sol/Terra/Luna), pricing confirmed across sources, July 9 GA launch, Altman 54% efficiency claim repeated in multiple outlets.

blurb_en:
The New York Times and a coalition of newspapers asked a federal judge Thursday to sanction OpenAI, accusing the company of lying to the court for two years about its ability to search its own systems for evidence of copyright infringement.
• The papers say a recent OpenAI employee deposition directly contradicted the company's earlier claim that it could not search its language models for copyrighted content.
• Allegation: OpenAI had already searched its systems before the first newspaper filed its lawsuit — and never disclosed it.
• OpenAI's defense: log access restrictions exist to protect user privacy.
• If successful, the sanctions motion could limit what evidence OpenAI is allowed to present at the main trial.
• The company that built its business on reading everyone else's words is now accused of refusing to let a court read its own. Read more.
blurb_zh:
《纽约时报》等报社联盟周四向联邦法官提交动议，要求制裁OpenAI，指控其在版权诉讼中两年来持续向法庭说谎，称其无法搜索自身系统中的侵权证据。
• 报社称，OpenAI员工的最新证词与公司此前陈述直接矛盾。
• 指控：OpenAI在第一件起诉书提交前就已对自身系统进行过搜索——且从未披露。
• OpenAI辩称：限制日志访问是为了保护用户隐私。
• 如制裁动议成功，可能影响OpenAI在正式审判中可提交的证据范围。
• 这家靠阅读所有人的文字建立起来的公司，如今被指控不肯让法庭阅读它自己的。
verification_notes: Confirmed via Washington Post (July 9, 2026), cross-referenced with Al Jazeera, The Hill, US News, Spectrum Local News, The Next Web, Let's Data Science. Key facts: NYT, NY Daily News, and other newspapers filed sanctions motion July 9; OpenAI deposition contradicted earlier claims; allegation: OpenAI searched systems pre-lawsuit without disclosure; user privacy defense from OpenAI.

blurb_en:
Streaming service TIDAL announced it will stop paying royalties on fully AI-generated music starting July 15, becoming the most aggressive major platform yet to formally cut AI tracks off from the money pool.
• AI-generated tracks will still be playable, but will carry an "AI" badge and earn zero royalties or direct-to-fan revenue.
• The policy also bans AI music that impersonates existing artists — framed as an anti-fraud measure.
• TIDAL described the policy as a "living document" — company speak for "we'll tighten it when detection technology catches up."
• For context: Deezer reports that 44% of all daily uploads are now AI-generated, but they account for only 1-3% of actual streams.
• TIDAL was founded partly by artists to pay musicians more fairly. It turns out "AI musician" doesn't qualify.
blurb_zh:
流媒体平台TIDAL宣布，自7月15日起停止向全AI生成的音乐支付版税，成为迄今最强硬的主流平台。
• AI生成曲目仍可播放，但将被标注"AI"标签，不再获得版税或粉丝直接销售收入。
• 该政策同时禁止模仿现有艺术家的AI音乐，定性为反欺诈措施。
• TIDAL称该政策为"动态文件"——意即随着检测技术提升将进一步收紧。
• 参考数据：Deezer报告显示，每日上传内容44%已是AI生成，但实际播放量仅占1%-3%。
• TIDAL由Jay-Z等艺人创立，初衷之一是让音乐人获得更公平的报酬——看来"AI音乐人"不在此列。
verification_notes: Confirmed via TechCrunch (June 29, 2026) and Variety (June 29, 2026), cross-referenced with iMusician, Zinstrel substack, Forbes (July 6 follow-up on AI detection), saasrise, partofstyle. Key facts: announced June 29, effective July 15; no royalties for 100% AI-generated music; AI badge labeling; impersonation ban; applies to Tidal Upload. Deezer 44% AI upload stat cross-referenced with earlier confirmed data.

blurb_en:
xAI launched Grok 4.5 publicly Tuesday, billing it as an "Opus-class" coding model — while its own published benchmarks show the hallucination rate nearly doubled, from 25% on the previous version to 54%.
• Trained partly on real developer session data from Cursor, which SpaceX acquired for approximately $60 billion in June 2026.
• Priced at $2 input/$6 output per million tokens — significantly cheaper than Anthropic's Opus 4.8 at $5/$25.
• Ranks fourth on the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index, behind Claude Fable 5, GPT-5.5, and Claude Opus 4.8.
• Not yet available in the EU; rollout expected mid-July.
• "Opus-class at a fraction of the price" and "makes things up twice as often" are both accurate descriptions. Read more.
blurb_zh:
xAI周二公开发布Grok 4.5，将其定位为"Opus级"编程模型——但自家公布的基准测试显示，幻觉率从上一版本的25%飙升至54%，几乎翻了一番。
• 训练数据部分来自Cursor平台的真实开发者会话数据——SpaceX于2026年6月以约600亿美元收购了Cursor。
• 定价为每百万令牌$2输入/$6输出，远低于Anthropic Opus 4.8的$5/$25。
• 在Artificial Analysis智能指数中排名第四，落后于Claude Fable 5、GPT-5.5和Claude Opus 4.8。
• 暂未在欧盟上线，预计7月中旬推出。
• "Opus级、价格打对折"和"每两次回答就可能有一次在编"，两句话都准确。
verification_notes: Launch confirmed via Axios (July 8, 2026) and TechCrunch (July 8, 2026). Key features confirmed across multiple sources. Hallucination rate (25% to 54%) from multiple aggregators citing xAI's own published benchmarks; specific numbers could not be independently confirmed from primary source due to paywalls but appeared consistently across sources. Cursor acquisition (~$60B) confirmed in earlier runs. $2/$6 pricing confirmed. Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index rank confirmed.
